title: ZIKA Antiviral Immunofluorescence staining Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e80ai","text":"This protocol describes immunofluorescence staining of fixed SARS-CoV-2 infected cells using IC7C7 primary antibody (produced in house against SARS-CoV-2 NP) and Alexa Fluor 488-conjugated secondary antibody. The procedure results in fluorescently labeled infected cells for quantification of viral infection.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"a4il1","text":"Please be sure to wear proper Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) while performing this experiment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Once you have the cells fixed with 10% PFA for minimum 24 hours you may handle the plates.
Remove PFA from the wells and discard in the fume hood.
Treat the wells with 100ul 0.1% Triton X-100-containing 1XPBS solution Contributed by users for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the triton, and wash 3x with 100ul 0.1% Tween 20 Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #170-6606-MSDS

Treat the wells with 50 ul primary flavivirus envelope protein antibody 4G2 (generated in house by Andrew.Duty@mssm.edu) at 0.001mg/ml ( 1:1000 ) in 1% BSA Incubate overnight at 4C. If urgent, incubate for 1-2 hours at RT
After incubation, wash 2x with 100ul 0.1% Tween 20 Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #170-6606-MSDS

Treat the wells with 50ul secondary antibody Goat anti-Rabbit IgG (H L) Cross-Adsorbed Secondary Antibody, Alexa Fluor™ 488 Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #A-11008 (1:1000) and DAPI in 1% BSA Incubate overnight at 4C. If urgent, incubate 1-2 hours at RT
After incubation, wash 2x with 100ul 0.1% Tween 20 Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #170-6606-MSDS
Add 100ul plain PBS to the wells for reading and storage
creator: Briana L McGovern
